source: next build output (this branch); measured, not aspirational
notes: Budgets are first-load JS (kB) reported by `next build`. The CI build step (.github/workflows/ci.yml) prints current sizes on every PR so regressions are visible in the log; automated enforcement is a follow-up.

total_static_js_kb.note: Sum of .next/static/chunks/**/*.js (raw), EXCLUDING route-isolated VECTOR game chunks which are budgeted separately under route_isolated_game_js_kb (owner decision 2026-07-19). Enforced in CI by scripts/check-bundle-budget.mjs; raise deliberately on an intended growth.

/command.note: Measured 434 kB when enforcement was introduced (wave 11.2) — pre-existing weight, not a wave regression; top code-splitting candidate (Console widget fan-out). Budget pinned just above measurement so further growth fails CI.

route_isolated_game_js_kb.note: Sum of dynamically imported VECTOR game chunks and their declared engine-vendor chunks. These load only when a user opens that specific game and are never part of another route's payload, so they are budgeted apart from the shared application bundle — bounded, not exempt. Sized to admit one Phaser build (~1168 kB raw), one Three build (~356 kB raw), and the nine game chunks. Per-route first-load budgets in check-perf-budgets.mjs remain the gate on what a user actually downloads.

watch: Fund/Console widgets: prefer cache-first over live per-item provider fan-out on first paint (audit N+1).
Heavy modules (/vitality 391kB) are candidates for route-level code splitting / dynamic import.
Do not cache financially material data without the visible freshness model (FreshnessBadge).
